etymology.description: semantic 言(言) (yán, "word; speech") + phonetic 射(射) (shè)
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 言(言) (yán, "word; speech") to convey the idea of verbal expression or saying something, with 射(射) (shè) providing the sound. This combination produces the sense of giving thanks through words.
etymology.simplification: 謝(謝) was simplified to 谢(謝) by replacing 言(言) (yán, "word; speech") with its simplified form 讠(讠).

examples.chinese: 谢谢你的帮助(謝謝你的幫助)。
她向我道了一声谢(她向我道了一聲謝)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiàng wǒ dàole yī shēng xiè.

examples.chinese: 我们应该感谢大自然给予的礼物(我們應該感謝大自然給予的禮物)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi gǎnxiè dàzìrán jǐyǔ de lǐwù.
examples.english: We should thank nature for its gifts.
